---
format: html
title: compute_dissimilarity_matrix()
---

<html><head>
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=yes" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.css">
<script type="text/javascript">
const macros = { "\\R": "\\textsf{R}", "\\mbox": "\\text", "\\code": "\\texttt"};
function processMathHTML() {
    var l = document.getElementsByClassName('reqn');
    for (let e of l) { katex.render(e.textContent, e, { throwOnError: false, macros }); }
    return;
}</script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.js"
    onload="processMathHTML();"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="R.css" />
</head><body><div class="container"><main>

<table style="width: 100%;"><tr><td>compute_dissimilarity_matrix</td><td style="text-align: right;">R Documentation</td></tr></table>

<h2>Compute Dissimilarity Matrix from Trait Data</h2>

<h3>Description</h3>

<p>Computes a pairwise dissimilarity matrix from a wide trait
table using 'cluster::daisy()'. Before computation, any
'Inf' / '-Inf' values in numeric trait columns are replaced
with 'NA' so that the normalisation denominator stays
finite. After computation, any remaining 'NaN' or non-finite
values in the distance matrix are replaced with '1.0' (the
maximum dissimilarity, i.e. fully dissimilar). This function
isolates the distance-computation step from
'cluster_functional_types()' so that the distance matrix can
be stored as an independent, inspectable pipeline target.
</p>


<h3>Usage</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>compute_dissimilarity_matrix(data, taxon_col = "taxon_name", metric = "gower")
</code></pre>


<h3>Arguments</h3>

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td><code id="data">data</code></td>
<td>
<p>A data frame with one row per taxon. Must contain a column
identified by 'taxon_col' and at least one additional trait
column. 'NA' values in trait columns are handled natively by
'cluster::daisy()'.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="taxon_col">taxon_col</code></td>
<td>
<p>A single character string naming the column that holds taxon
names. Default: '&quot;taxon_name&quot;'. This column is excluded before
the distance computation.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="metric">metric</code></td>
<td>
<p>A single character string passed to 'cluster::daisy()' as the
'metric' argument. Default: '&quot;gower&quot;'. Other valid values are
'&quot;euclidean&quot;' and '&quot;manhattan&quot;' (see '?cluster::daisy').</p>
</td></tr>
</table>


<h3>Details</h3>

<p>**Steps performed**:
</p>

<ol>
<li><p> Select all columns except 'taxon_col' as trait columns.
</p>
</li>
<li><p> Replace any 'Inf' / '-Inf' values in numeric trait
columns with 'NA' via 'dplyr::if_else()'.
</p>
</li>
<li><p> Compute 'cluster::daisy(metric = metric)'.
</p>
</li>
<li><p> If any value in the resulting distance vector is
non-finite (e.g. 'NaN' arising when two taxa share no
non-'NA' trait), replace it with '1.0' and reconvert via
'stats::as.dist()'.
</p>
</li></ol>



<h3>Value</h3>

<p>An object of class '&quot;dist&quot;' (as returned by 'stats::as.dist()')
with one entry per pair of taxa. All values are in [0, 1] when
'metric = &quot;gower&quot;' (Gower default).
</p>


<h3>See Also</h3>

<p>[fit_hclust()], [cluster_functional_types()]
</p>

</main>

</div>
</body></html>
